top_preview.heading_path: OpenClaw Mem Engine (slot backend) — Design + Roadmap / Retrieval design (the part you’ll feel) / Docs memory (decisions/roadmaps/specs) as a cold lane
top_preview.title: Docs memory (decisions/roadmaps/specs) as a cold lane
top_preview.text:
Operators feel recall failure most painfully on: “we already decided this.”

So the engine path should be able to **optionally** search an operator-authored docs corpus (DECISIONS / roadmaps / specs) using the same hybrid recipe (FTS + embeddings) and return bounded citations.

Status (current):
- shipped as `docsColdLane` in `openclaw-mem-engine` config
- installable tools: `memory_docs_ingest`, `memory_docs_search`
- `memory_recall` + `autoRecall` can consult cold lane only after hot lane is insufficient (`minHotItems`)
- results are marked `source_kind=operator`, `trust_tier=trusted`
- embeddings are optional/fail-open (FTS-only still works)

- Spec: Docs memory hybrid search v0 (maintainer archive; not part of the public evaluator path)
- Ops guide: [mem-engine-admin-ops.md](mem-engine-admin-ops.md#docs-memory-cold-lane-installable)
- Operational hardening note: scoped docs search starvation root cause and verifier (maintainer archive)
- Next design slice: docs cold lane scope pushdown v1 (maintainer archive)
- Stance: **no local LLM**; rerank (if needed) is remote + bounded.

---

top_preview.heading_path: 2026-03-21 — Strategy powerhouse learning-closure priority after TradingAgents review / What "learning closure" should mean in Steamer
top_preview.title: What "learning closure" should mean in Steamer
top_preview.text:
The target is **not** raw prompt-memory injection.
It is a governed closure loop with explicit writeback and retrieval surfaces.

More precisely:
- convert important nightly outcomes, replay contradictions, operator corrections, and promotion/hold/reject decisions into durable lessons
- attach those lessons to a bounded retrieval surface keyed by strategy family / regime / failure mode / evidence class
- make future research, triage, and validation packets consume those lessons deliberately
- keep the human/operator gate and canonical daily truth separate from any LLM narrative convenience layer

In other words:
- **learn from the evidence chain**
- do **not** just paste prior prose back into the next prompt and pretend that is memory

top_preview.title: Promotion rule
top_preview.text:
If a memory seems bigger than room-local chat continuity, do **not** store it as room memory.
Instead:
- either keep it unstored,
- or escalate to Lyria using `escalation-judge` if it may deserve durable/shared treatment.
